title: Memory Pagination
summary: Describes memory pagination: pages, page frames, page tables, address translation, multi-level tables, and the TLB.
content_points: Virtual address space and physical memory divided into equal-size pages/frames (typically 4 KB).
Page table (per process) maps virtual pages to physical frames.
Address translation: page_number = virtual_addr / page_size; offset = virtual_addr % page_size; physical = frame * page_size + offset.
32-bit address space with 4 KB pages → 2^20 entries per table → too large; solution: multi-level tables (Intel: Page Directory + Page Table).
TLB: small, fast cache inside the CPU for recent page translations. Invalidated on context switch.
Internal fragmentation: unused space within pages; less severe than external fragmentation.
learning_objectives: Describe the TLB and its invalidation
Compare internal and external memory fragmentation
